title: Wrong UUIDs in SERVICES — monitoring blind
symptom: Cilly doesn't see bot errors, logs always empty, autofix never fires
root_cause: The SERVICES dict had hardcoded wrong Railway service UUIDs. The API returned 404, the error was swallowed — monitoring silently did nothing
why_architecture: Cilly is the single monitoring layer. If SERVICES holds wrong IDs, the whole office is blind. No startup validation → the bug lives unnoticed for weeks
fix: Replace hardcoded UUIDs with a fetch from the Railway GraphQL API: project→services→edges→node→id
prevention: When adding a new bot: take UUIDs only from the Railway API, never copy by hand. Add a SERVICES liveness check at Cilly startup
cause: Service UUIDs in SERVICES were hardcoded by hand and went stale after a Railway restart

title: environment ID passed as the string 'production' instead of a UUID
symptom: serviceInstanceRedeploy returned an error, deploy didn't start
root_cause: The Railway GraphQL API requires a UUID for environmentId. The string 'production' is a human-readable name, not an identifier
why_architecture: Railway distinguishes an environment name from its UUID. The name can change, the UUID can't. Using names instead of IDs makes code fragile
fix: Replace the string 'production' with the UUID: 2efaaf60-3568-4462-8b77-f4a7e3c65b49
prevention: All Railway operations: always use UUIDs (projectId, environmentId, serviceId). Store them in constants, not strings
cause: The Railway GraphQL API requires environmentId as a UUID, not a string alias

title: New bot not added to SERVICES — monitoring doesn't see it
symptom: A bot crashes and recovers, Cilly doesn't react
root_cause: SERVICES is an explicit whitelist of services to monitor. A new bot is not added there automatically
why_architecture: The architecture requires manual registration of every new service. That's a forgetting point — when creating a bot people think about code, not monitoring
fix: Add service_id to SERVICES: {uuid: (repo_name, main_file)}
prevention: Bot creation checklist: 1) BotFather 2) Railway service 3) GitHub repo 4) SERVICES in coder.py 5) BOT_URLS in filly-bot. Without step 4 — monitoring is blind
cause: The bot-creation checklist did not include the mandatory step of adding service_id to SERVICES

title: Unescaped Markdown special chars → 400 Bad Request when sending a lesson
symptom: Cilly cannot send a message with code or special chars to Telegram
root_cause: parse_mode=Markdown requires escaping _, *, [, ] and other chars. Code and file paths contain them unescaped
why_architecture: Cilly generates technical content (paths, code, stacktraces) and sends it straight to Telegram. These two domains are incompatible without intermediate processing
fix: Drop parse_mode or switch to HTML tags (<code>, <b>). HTML escaping is safer for technical content
prevention: For technical content: always HTML parse_mode or plain text. Markdown — only for controlled content without special chars
cause: Telegram Bot API parse_mode=MarkdownV2 requires escaping special chars; LLM-generated code doesn't escape them

title: Critical operations at module level → crash on import
symptom: App crashes at startup with ValueError: missing env variable
root_cause: get_required_env() and get_port() were called at module level (outside functions). Python runs this code at import, before env vars may be set
why_architecture: Railway sets env vars before launching the process, but if code reads them at import (in tests or during deploy) the order can break
fix: Move config init into main(). Wrap in try/except/finally for graceful shutdown
prevention: Rule: no side effects at module level. Anything reading env or opening connections — only inside functions
cause: Calling os.getenv() or validation at module level runs at import, before env vars are loaded

title: global variable in a function creates a local reference
symptom: NameError in an async handler: variable not defined, even though initialized in main()
root_cause: The global keyword without assignment in a child function doesn't grant access to the global if it was initialized in another async function
why_architecture: The async boilerplate uses globals (redis_client, ptb_bot) to pass state between main() and handlers. That's fragile — init order is non-obvious
fix: Use context.bot_data or pass dependencies via parameters. Or explicit global + assignment in main()
prevention: In a bot template: declare all globals at the top of the file as None, init in main() with explicit global. Or move to dependency injection
cause: Python: if a variable is used with global inside a function, it becomes local across the whole function scope

title: Unhandled exception in the main thread → silent crash
symptom: Bot crashes with no traceback in logs, unexpected shutdown
root_cause: app.run_polling() without try/except. An exception in the main thread ends the process without logging
why_architecture: Railway restarts the container on crash, but without a traceback the cause is impossible to find. Cilly's monitoring sees CRASHED but not why
fix: Wrap app.run_polling() in try/except with logging.exception()
prevention: In a bot template: all of main() in try/except. Log every unhandled exception before exit
cause: PTB app.run_polling() wasn't wrapped in try/except, an unhandled exception killed the main thread without a traceback

title: Aggressive httpx timeouts → crash on unstable network
symptom: httpx.ReadError, timeout crash, bot falls over on connection loss
root_cause: python-telegram-bot defaults to a 5-second timeout with no retry logic. Railway's network is unstable during deploys
why_architecture: Bots run on Railway where the network can drop during neighboring-service deploys. Without retry — any transient blip = crash
fix: Raise timeouts: read/write/connect to 30s each. Add exponential backoff for retry
prevention: In the template: Application.builder().read_timeout(30).write_timeout(30). All HTTP clients — with retry logic
cause: httpx.AsyncClient was created with the default timeout (5s), insufficient for slow Railway endpoints

title: Telethon: f.title is TextWithEntities, not str
symptom: tg_add_peer_to_folder crashes: TextWithEntities has no attribute lower
root_cause: Telethon 1.3x+ returns a TextWithEntities object for DialogFilter.title instead of a string
why_architecture: Cilly uses Telethon for folder/group operations. The Telethon API changed and isn't always backward compatible
fix: Use (f.title.text if hasattr(f.title, 'text') else str(f.title)).strip().lower()
prevention: When working with Telethon objects: always hasattr-check before calling string methods
cause: Telethon returns TextWithEntities objects instead of str for fields like title

title: InviteToChannelRequest doesn't work for plain Chat groups
symptom: Cannot cast InputPeerChat to any kind of InputChannel
root_cause: InviteToChannelRequest works only for Channel/Supergroup. A plain Chat requires AddChatUserRequest
why_architecture: Telegram distinguishes Chat (legacy) and Channel/Supergroup. The Office group (-5194783850) is a plain Chat. Cilly must know the group type
fix: isinstance check: Channel → InviteToChannelRequest, Chat → AddChatUserRequest(chat_id, user, fwd_limit=0)
prevention: Before Telegram group operations: check the type via isinstance(entity, Channel)
cause: The Telegram API separates Channel and Chat — plain groups (Chat) need AddChatUserRequest, not InviteToChannelRequest

title: Double-escaped quotes in BOT_TEMPLATE → SyntaxError when the generated bot runs
symptom: A bot generated by Cilly crashes at startup: SyntaxError on a triple-quoted line
root_cause: Triple-quoted strings in BOT_TEMPLATE get double-escaped when formatted via .format(). The result is invalid Python
why_architecture: Cilly generates code and pushes it without a syntax check. The error is found only at deploy
fix: Use single quotes for the SYSTEM string in the template. Add ast.parse() validation before push
prevention: push_file() for .py files: always ast.parse() before sending. Already implemented in shared/github_tools.py
cause: The bot template was generated via an f-string with double quotes — quotes in SYSTEM_PROMPT broke Python syntax

title: Railway URL derived from Telegram username → wrong domain
symptom: URL kriss-ai-office-bot-production instead of kriss-bot-production
root_cause: The bot URL was derived from the Telegram username (@kriss_ai_office_bot → kriss-ai-office-bot). The Railway domain is based on the service name, not the username
why_architecture: Two namespaces: Telegram usernames and Railway service names — unrelated and can diverge
fix: Take the URL only from the Railway GraphQL API (query services → domain) or from the hardcoded BOT_URLS in filly-bot
prevention: Never derive a Railway URL from a Telegram username. Source of truth — BOT_URLS in filly-bot/bot.py
cause: The Railway internal URL was built from bot.username (Telegram handle) instead of serviceId from the Railway API

title: CHAT_PROMPT lacked an infra map — Cilly asked the obvious
symptom: Cilly asked 'which repo?', 'where are the bots registered?' for basic tasks
root_cause: CHAT_PROMPT had general instructions but didn't know the concrete office architecture: repositories, BOT_URLS, group IDs
why_architecture: Cilly is the office's operational agent. Without an infra map every task needs clarification, which blocks autonomy
fix: Add the full office map to CHAT_PROMPT: repos, filly-bot/bot.py as the BOT_URLS source, group IDs, Telethon functions
prevention: When expanding the office (new bot, new project) — update CHAT_PROMPT and SYSTEM_STATE.md together
cause: CHAT_PROMPT held only a general task description, without a concrete bot→repo→URL mapping

title: Duplicate keys in the BOT_URLS dict — routing to the wrong URL
symptom: The bot routes to the last of the duplicate URLs; the earlier ones are silently ignored. Invisible without an explicit check
root_cause: Python dict: on a duplicate key the last value wins. The key 'KRIS' was written twice with different URLs
why_architecture: BOT_URLS is the office's central router in filly-bot. Any error there affects all routing. No startup validation
fix: Remove duplicate keys. Add assert len(BOT_URLS) == len(set(BOT_URLS)) at startup
prevention: When adding a bot to BOT_URLS: search by key before inserting. A linter (pylint W0109) catches dups statically
cause: A Python dict with duplicate keys silently overwrites the previous value, no error

title: time.sleep() in async context blocks the whole event loop
symptom: The bot freezes for 2-8s on every Anthropic 529 retry. Other messages aren't processed
root_cause: A sync function with time.sleep() was called from an async handler. time.sleep() blocks the whole event loop — all bots in the process freeze
why_architecture: Python asyncio is cooperative multitasking. One blocking call = the whole office stalls. Especially critical for retry logic that may wait seconds
fix: Make it async def, replace time.sleep → await asyncio.sleep, use AsyncAnthropic
prevention: In async projects: never time.sleep, requests, or other blocking calls. For Anthropic — AsyncAnthropic. For HTTP — httpx.AsyncClient or aiohttp
cause: time.sleep() blocks the entire event loop — in async context you need await asyncio.sleep()

title: New bot not added to Cilly's SERVICES — monitoring blind (gosling)
symptom: gosling-bot crashed and recovered, Cilly didn't react and wrote no lessons
root_cause: gosling-bot's service_id wasn't in the SERVICES dict. Cilly monitors only registered services
why_architecture: A duplicate of lesson #3 — a systemic problem. No automatic registration when a bot is created
fix: Add ed03c9d3 to SERVICES
prevention: The bot-creation checklist is mandatory (see lesson #3). Consider auto-registration: on deploy of a new service Cilly adds it to SERVICES herself
cause: gosling-bot was added to the office without updating SERVICES in Cilly's coder.py

title: IGNORE_PATTERNS applied to the whole log block — real errors masked
symptom: A real bug went unnoticed if a TelegramConflictError (in IGNORE_PATTERNS) was nearby in the logs
root_cause: any(pattern in all_logs) looks at the whole block. If a 50-line block has one ignored line — the whole block is ignored, including real errors
why_architecture: The monitor loop reads logs in blocks. Filtering at block level instead of line level is a systemic granularity error
fix: Apply IGNORE_PATTERNS only to error_logs (lines with errors), not to the whole block
prevention: Log filters: always per-line, not per-block. Test: artificially insert a real error next to an ignored line and check it's not dropped
cause: IGNORE_PATTERNS was filtered across the whole log block (contains), not per line — one noisy log hid a real bug next to it

title: serviceInstanceRedeploy doesn't work without a prior SUCCESS deploy
symptom: redeploy returns true, but no new deploy appears. The service hangs in FAILED
root_cause: serviceInstanceRedeploy restarts the last SUCCESSFUL deploy. If all deploys are FAILED — there's nothing to restart
why_architecture: Railway redeploy ≠ a new deploy. An important distinction Cilly must know for autofix
fix: If there are no SUCCESS deploys — use variableCollectionUpsert (_CACHE_BUST) to trigger a new deploy
prevention: Before redeploy: check there is at least one SUCCESS deploy. If not — variableCollectionUpsert
cause: serviceInstanceRedeploy in the Railway API requires an existing successful deploy to create a new one

title: Cilly pushed invalid Python → CRASHED for 2+ days
symptom: cilly-bot status CRASHED, unresponsive, no audits run
root_cause: The LLM via generate_fix produced agents/coder.py with a SyntaxError (four quotes on line 262). push_file didn't check syntax
why_architecture: Cilly can break herself. This is the single critical risk in the architecture — if coder.py is invalid, the whole office has no brain
fix: Add ast.parse() validation in push_file() for all .py files BEFORE push
prevention: push_file() for .py: ast.parse() mandatory. Already implemented. Also: keep a backup of the last working coder.py in Redis
cause: push_file() pushed Python code without a syntax check — an invalid file went to prod

title: json.loads() without try/except on an LLM response → nightly crash
symptom: vietnam-bot crashes every night at 01:00 UTC
root_cause: The LLM sometimes returns an explanation instead of clean JSON. json.loads() on such a response → ValueError → unhandled exception in the scheduler
why_architecture: The LLM is non-deterministic — it can return anything. Any json.loads(llm_response) without protection is a potential crash point
fix: try/except around json.loads() + guard: if there's no '[' in the response → fall back to defaults
prevention: RULE: never json.loads() an LLM response without try/except. Always extract JSON by finding brackets + try/except
cause: The Anthropic API sometimes returns JSON with a trailing comma or markdown wrapper — json.loads() without try/except kills the process

title: Substring match of a bot name → replies to unrelated words ('basically' for 'billy')
symptom: Bots replied to messages where any word containing the bot name appeared
root_cause: should_respond_in_group() used BOT_NAME.lower() in msg_text — substring match instead of word-boundary
why_architecture: Russian bot names (billy, milly, tilly) are common substrings. Substring match is unusable for names in text
fix: re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(BOT_NAME.lower()) + r'\b', msg_text) — word-boundary regex
prevention: To detect names in text: always word-boundary regex, never substring. Applies to all bots in group mode
cause: str.find() or 'name in text' matches a substring — 'billy' is found inside 'debilly' and other words

title: AttributeError on non-text messages in a group
symptom: Bots crashed when receiving a photo/sticker/voice in a group
root_cause: handle_message() didn't check update.message and update.message.text. In DMs the filters.TEXT filter protected it, in a group — not
why_architecture: A group receives all message types. Extending a bot into a group needs extra guards that aren't in the template
fix: Guard at the start of handle_message: if not update.message or not update.message.text: return
prevention: Group-mode template: always guard on message.text at the very start of the handler
cause: A PTB Update may contain non-text messages — accessing update.message.text without a type check kills the bot

title: Startup notification on every env var change → spam
symptom: Watchdog sent a 'bot started' notification on every deploy, including env var changes
root_cause: Railway deploys on every env var change. A startup notification in code = a message on every deploy
why_architecture: Batching changes in Railway is impossible — each variable change triggers a deploy. Bots with frequent env changes shouldn't have startup notifications
fix: Remove the startup tg() message. Notifications only on real events
prevention: Rule: no startup Telegram notifications in bots that may restart often. Batch env changes into one operation
cause: The startup notification in on_startup was sent on every restart — each env var change triggers a Railway redeploy

title: _client undefined in handle_task (mama-bot)
symptom: Ellice returned NameError on any request from Filly via /task
root_cause: _client is a parameter of _call_llm(), not a global. handle_task used the wrong reference
why_architecture: The bot template uses a global claude object. If handle_task uses a different variable — a silent bug until the first call
fix: Replace _client.messages.create() with claude.messages.create() in handle_task
prevention: In HTTP handlers use only claude (the global client) or process(). Call _call_llm only inside process()
cause: The HTTP handler used _client (a Telethon global) instead of a local anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()

title: message.text = None for non-text group messages → TypeError
symptom: Bots crashed with TypeError on a photo/sticker/voice in a group
root_cause: After adding a group, message.text can be None. msg = update.message.text without a check → TypeError on the next line
why_architecture: A duplicate of lesson #22 with a different manifestation. Systemic problem: the template isn't group-ready
fix: if not msg: return — right after msg = update.message.text
prevention: The template must include a None guard for message.text. Add to new_bot_template.py
cause: Telegram delivers photos/stickers/voices as separate Update types — message.text = None for non-text messages

title: Image generation in a group on a random keyword match
symptom: Bots launched expensive image generation on a random group reply
root_cause: wants_image(msg) didn't check is_group. With a 30% reply chance in a group, the bot could start generation on the word 'draw' from anyone
why_architecture: Side effects (expensive ops) shouldn't depend on a random group reply. That violates the principle of least surprise
fix: if wants_image(msg) and not is_group: — image generation only in private chats
prevention: When extending a bot into a group: audit all side effects. Expensive/irreversible ops — only on explicit request, not a random reply
cause: wants_image() detection was keyword-based — in a group any member could accidentally trigger generation

title: checkin_scheduler: replace(day=day+1) → missed message + ValueError at month end
symptom: Doctor didn't send the morning message. On a service restart after 06:00 UTC the checkin was skipped to the next day
root_cause: target.replace(day=target.day+1) — on a restart after the trigger time the scheduler moved to day+1. Plus: replace(day=32) → ValueError at month end
why_architecture: asyncio schedulers start when the process starts. Railway restarts services (deploys, watchdog). A scheduler must survive a restart at any time of day
fix: target += timedelta(days=1). Commit: 983b2c3ba3 in unperson22-alt/doctor-bot
prevention: For dates always timedelta, never replace(day=N+1). Test schedulers by simulating a restart after the trigger time
cause: datetime.replace(day=day+1) fails at month end and doesn't roll the month — use timedelta

title: /post_raw ignored bot_name — all messages came from Cilly
symptom: When sending on behalf of different bots, all messages arrived from Cilly's account, only the text differed
root_cause: /post_raw took a bot_name param but didn't use it — it always sent via Cilly's token. bot_name was decorative
why_architecture: In Telegram each bot = a separate account with its own token. For a message to come from Billy you need Billy's token. One endpoint can't send on behalf of all bots
fix: Add a /send endpoint to each bot (uses its own token). Cilly's /post_raw now proxies the request to the target bot's /send via BOT_URLS
prevention: To send on behalf of a specific bot: always proxy the request to that bot, never use someone else's token. Architectural principle: each bot owns its token
cause: /post_raw took a bot_name param but ignored it — it used the global bot object instead of a name lookup

title: for bot in [...] makes bot a local across the whole async function
symptom: UnboundLocalError: cannot access local variable 'bot' — on calling bot.send_message in the same function that has for bot in [...] somewhere below
root_cause: Python compiles the whole function at once. Any assignment to a variable (including a for loop variable) makes it local EVERYWHERE in the function — even before the assignment line
why_architecture: handle_natural_language is a big function (3000+ lines) with dozens of variables. The name clash between the global bot object and the loop variable is non-obvious and not caught by a linter without running
fix: Rename: for bot in [...] → for bot_key in [...]. Commit b603a91a1d
prevention: Reserved global object names (bot, redis, claude) never used as loop variables or locals inside functions that use the global
cause: Python: a for-loop variable for bot in [...] shadows the global bot in the same scope

title: A bot can't delete a user's DM message via the Bot API
symptom: A message with a key/secret stayed in the DM after the bot replied
root_cause: Bot API deleteMessage in a private chat works only for the bot's own messages. Deleting a user's message requires the user themselves or a Telethon userbot (MTProto)
why_architecture: Office bots use the Bot API, not MTProto. Operations requiring user rights need a separate Telethon client via get_telethon_client()
fix: Use a Telethon userbot: tg_cl = await get_telethon_client() → get_messages(bot_entity) → delete_messages(). The bot finds the dialog with itself via the bot's entity
prevention: Any deletion of others' messages — only via Telethon. Bot API deleteMessage works only for the bot's own messages
cause: Telegram Bot API deleteMessage works only for the bot's own messages — others' messages can be deleted only by a Telethon userbot (MTProto)

symptom: Cilly can't find the bot-name repo for tasks involving marketing or trading bots
cause: Bots in dept monorepos live in folders, not separate repositories
context: ray-bot задача завершилась 404 — unperson22-alt/ray-bot не существует
fix: Marketing bots: unperson22-alt/marketing-dept/ray/, marty/, nelli/, copy/, lex/. Trading: unperson22-alt/trading-dept. If a bot isn't found as a repo — look in the dept monorepos.
prevention: Before a 404 — check the marketing-dept and trading-dept monorepos
title: dept monorepos: bots live in folders, not separate repositories

symptom: GitHub API returns 404 when creating a file — the URL has a duplicated org: unperson22-alt/unperson22-alt/...
cause: Cilly builds the path as org + full_repo_path instead of just repo_name/path
context: Задача создать family-dept/README.md — Силли обратилась по URL /repos/unperson22-alt/unperson22-alt/contents/family-dept/README.md
fix: GitHub API path: /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path}. owner=unperson22-alt, repo=family-dept, path=README.md. Never include owner in repo or path.
prevention: Before a GitHub API call check: repo holds only the repo name (no org), path doesn't start with org or repo.
title: GitHub API path: owner/repo/contents/path, not org/bot-name

symptom: HTTP /task returns 500 on messages longer than ~200 chars
cause: 1) max_tokens=1024 — Claude truncates the response on long tasks. 2) r.content[0].text — fails if Claude returned a tool_use block instead of text. 3) No try/except in handle_task
fix: 1) max_tokens 1024→4096. 2) text = next((b.text for b in r.content if hasattr(b, 'text')), '[no text]'). 3) try/except in handle_task. NOTE: client = anthropic.Anthropic() is synchronous, no await needed.
prevention: Before adding await, check the client type: Anthropic() = sync, AsyncAnthropic() = async. Don't change await without checking the init.
title: max_tokens=1024 truncates the response + correct parsing of content blocks

symptom: All bots on ai-office-shared v0.1.9 crash with ImportError at startup
cause: An auto-import of impact_client (which uses 'requests') was added to __init__.py — but requests isn't a package dependency. Any from ai_office_shared.shared import X crashes the whole package.
fix: 1) Remove the auto-import from __init__.py. 2) In impact_client.py replace requests with httpx (httpx is already present in all bots). 3) Bump to v0.1.10. 4) Update requirements.txt in all bots.
prevention: Before adding an import to __init__.py check: are all of the new module's deps in the package pyproject.toml? Never do an eager import in __init__.py for modules with external deps.
title: Auto-import in __init__.py takes down all bots when a dependency is missing

symptom: Cilly searches for repos with wrong names (ai-office, silly-bot, silly-office) when specific bots are needed
cause: Cilly doesn't know the bot → repository mapping
fix: Registry: billy→billy-bot, kriss→kriss-bot, milly→milly-bot, villy→villy-bot, gosling→gosling-bot, ellis/mama→mama-bot, prophet→prophet-bot, pilly→pilly-bot, dilly/doctor→dilly-bot, tilly→tilly-bot, filly→filly-bot, sillie→cilly-bot (c). Marketing: marketing-dept/ray/, marketing-dept/nelli/, marketing-dept/marty/ etc. Trading: trading-dept/. Family: family-dept/.
prevention: Before searching for a file in a repo — check this registry. The repo is named {bot}-bot, not {bot}-office or ai-{bot}.
title: A bot→repo registry is mandatory in Cilly's CHAT_PROMPT

symptom: Cilly searches for RAILWAY_TOKEN in GitHub repos (silli-config, silli-tools) and doesn't find it
cause: Cilly doesn't know the Railway token is available as an env var via os.getenv, not stored in GitHub
fix: RAILWAY_TOKEN_VLAD is available via os.getenv('RAILWAY_TOKEN_VLAD'). coder.py already has RAILWAY_TOKEN = os.getenv('RAILWAY_TOKEN_VLAD') or os.getenv('RAILWAY_TOKEN'). For the Railway API use railway_graphql(query).
prevention: Never search for secrets/tokens in GitHub repos. All tokens — in Railway env vars. Check via os.getenv().
title: RAILWAY_TOKEN is available via os.getenv, not from GitHub

symptom: Cilly writes every intermediate task step to the office — clutters the chat
cause: No rule for when to write to the office
fix: Write to the office ONLY the final report (success or error). Intermediate steps — logs only.
prevention: Rule: call send_to_group() at most 1-2 times per task — at the start (if the task is long) and at the end.
title: send_to_group() only the final result, not intermediate steps
confidence as a string instead of a number
bug: float(intent_data.get("confidence", 1.0)) падает с ValueError если Claude вернул "low"/"medium"/"high" вместо числа
fix: isinstance check + mapping: {"high":0.9,"medium":0.6,"low":0.3}.get(str(v).lower(),0.5)
why_architecture: Claude doesn't guarantee a field's type in a JSON response — always protect parsing with a fallback
symptom: The LLM returns confidence as a string ('high'/'medium'/'low') — the code expected a float → TypeError on comparison
cause: The Claude API doesn't guarantee a field's type — a JSON field may be a string or a number depending on the prompt
prevention: For any numeric field from the LLM: isinstance check + map strings to numbers

title: web_search cuts off the response on stop_reason=tool_use
bug: process() делал один вызов к Claude. Если Claude использовал web_search — stop_reason=tool_use, text пустой или обрезанный
fix: Multi-turn loop: after tool_use collect tool_result blocks and call again until stop_reason=end_turn
why_architecture: Any tool call (tools=[...]) requires multi-turn — a single call works only for simple responses without tool_use
symptom: web_search tool_use cuts off the response — stop_reason=tool_use, no final text
cause: The Anthropic API stops on tool_use and waits for tool_result — a single call doesn't get the final answer
prevention: When using tools: always a multi-turn loop until stop_reason=end_turn

title: HTTP server didn't start — unreachable code after return
symptom: bot:TIMEOUT on health check. SSL handshake passes, no HTTP response at all.
cause: The HTTP startup (app_http, runner, TCPSite, ptb) sat inside a handler function AFTER return — unreachable code. main() ended right after 'Redis connected' without starting the HTTP server.
context: kriss-bot. При рефакторинге handle_reply() вставили перед HTTP-стартом. Railway деплой показывает SUCCESS даже если порт не слушается.
fix: Move app_http/runner/TCPSite/ptb into main(). Handler functions must be pure — only request handling. Commit 61a3deb2.
how_to_avoid: После любого рефакторинга bot.py — проверять что весь HTTP-старт и PTB инициализация всегда внутри main(). SSL connect без HTTP ответа = HTTP сервер не запущен (не Railway outage). Railway SUCCESS не гарантирует что порт слушается.
prevention: Start the HTTP layer (app_http, runner, TCPSite, ptb) after all init — no code after they launch

title: Cilly's monitoring covered 1 of 7 Railway projects — dept failures invisible
symptom: The whole trading-dept (5 bots) was down in FAILED, nobody noticed — neither Cilly nor watchdog alerted
root_cause: SERVICES/HEALTH_URLS is a static list of only the awake-happiness project. The depts (trading/medical/family/marketing/dev/vietnam) are separate Railway projects outside that list
why_architecture: The office = 7 Railway dept projects. Monitoring one project = blind spots on 6 of 7
fix: run_daily_audit: sweep ALL projects via the Railway projects query (as in ops.py), alert on non-SUCCESS in any dept
prevention: Build office monitoring from enumerating all projects (projects query), not from a static single-project list
cause: Monitoring was tied to a hardcoded list of one project's services

title: trading-dept wouldn't build: pinned python + mise github-attestations (railpack)
symptom: All 5 trading-dept bots in FAILED — the deploy fails at the build stage
root_cause: runtime.txt pinned python-3.12.7 → railpack/mise installs that version with a GitHub-attestation check; mise 2026.6.1 can't find them → build fail
why_architecture: Bots on railpack/mise (no Dockerfile) depend on mise's external build infra. Most bots use Dockerfiles — so only trading-dept was hit
fix: mise.toml: [settings] python.github_attestations=false (durable) or env MISE_PYTHON_GITHUB_ATTESTATIONS=false; or move to a Dockerfile
prevention: Prefer a Dockerfile over railpack/mise. If mise — keep a mise.toml with attestations disabled
cause: Railpack/mise couldn't verify attestations for the pinned python from runtime.txt

title: get_me() at startup without retry → service crash on a transient Telegram TimedOut
symptom: The bot built, but crashes at startup: telegram.error.TimedOut on await bot.get_me()
root_cause: Startup get_me() without retry. A transient network timeout to api.telegram.org kills the whole main() before the HTTP server starts
why_architecture: The Railway→Telegram network is unstable when several services start at once. A startup check must not be a single point of failure
fix: get_me in a retry loop with backoff + NOT fatal: after failure keep starting (the bot works via /analyze + /health)
prevention: Any startup Telegram call — with retry and non-fatal. A network drop at startup must not kill the service (related to lesson #8)
cause: Startup get_me() wasn't wrapped in retry/try, a timeout killed the process

title: Cross-project service on redis.railway.internal → Redis unreachable
symptom: After moving a service to another project: 'Redis unavailable: redis.railway.internal:6379 Name or service not known'
root_cause: REDIS_URL pointed at the private host redis.railway.internal. Railway private networking works ONLY within one project, and Redis lives in another
why_architecture: *.railway.internal is a project's private network. When a service moves between projects, internal references silently stop resolving
fix: Use the public Redis proxy URL (xxx.proxy.rlwy.net:port) for cross-project access
prevention: *.railway.internal — same-project only. A service reaching a resource in ANOTHER project must use the public URL
cause: Internal redis.railway.internal doesn't resolve from another Railway project

title: Agentic loop without an anti-spam guard + begging for secrets in chat → group flood
symptom: Cilly flooded the office group (~15 messages/min), repeatedly begging people for the Railway token
root_cause: agentic_task had no tool to read a Railway variable → the agent kept asking the group for the token in a loop; the stop guard was only on read_file errors, not send_message; there was no global stop flag
why_architecture: Agentic ReAct loops without outbound limiters = flood risk. Plus asking for secrets in chat is itself a leak
fix: Cap messages to the group per task + dedup; stop on a repeated action 3x; prompt ban on asking for secrets; kill-switch CILLY_PAUSED / /pause; a check_var action to read a variable
prevention: Agentic loops: cap on outbound to chat + dedup + stop-on-repeat; NEVER ask for tokens/secrets in chat; always have an instant mute
cause: No limit on posting to the group + no tool for the task → endless requests for help

title: error_signature on raw log lines → one bug re-detected, fix_count never fires
symptom: Cilly keeps 'finding' the same bug in a loop and proposing a fix, never reaching escalation (deep-diagnose)
root_cause: The error signature = md5 of the first 3 log lines. Different line numbers/dynamics → new signature → fix_count resets, the dedup cooldown is bypassed
why_architecture: Dedup/repeat counting is tied to the signature. An unstable signature breaks the whole anti-loop protection
fix: Stable signature: exception type + file name + message without numbers (normalized)
prevention: Build dedup signatures from stable fields, not from raw text with line numbers/addresses
cause: md5 over raw log lines is unstable because of line numbers

title: Hardcoded Railway/Redis tokens right in the repository code
symptom: Live Railway and Redis-proxy tokens sat as strings in .py — any repo clone = prod access
root_cause: Tokens were written as literals (scripts/ollama_switch.py, agents/coder.py, shared/*) instead of read from env
why_architecture: Secrets in git = a leak forever (history keeps them even after deletion). Especially dangerous for tokens with prod rights
fix: Read from os.environ; remove literals; .env in .gitignore; ROTATE leaked values
prevention: Secrets — only in env vars. Never commit tokens. A leaked value — rotate it (removing from the file isn't enough)
cause: Tokens were written into the code as strings instead of os.environ

title: bot.py overwritten by a one-off Railway provision script committed as bot.py → crash
symptom: billy-bot CRASHED on boot: KeyError 'RAILWAY_API_TOKEN' at module level. Cilly couldn't fix it — the whole file was foreign
root_cause: An executor wrote an env-var setup script OVER billy/bot.py and merged into main. The deployed bot.py became a provision script with os.environ[...] at module level
why_architecture: A bot's entrypoint (bot.py) is not a scratchpad. Overwriting it with an ops script kills the bot, and the autofixer can't fix it (it's not a logic bug, it's a file swap)
fix: Restore the real bot.py from a branch/history; keep provision/ops scripts as a separate file, not the entrypoint
prevention: Never use a bot's live repo/entrypoint as a scratchpad for ops scripts. push_file to bot.py — only the real bot code
cause: A one-off script was committed over bot.py and deployed

title: Screener took the OLDEST candle — BingX klines arrive newest-first
symptom: All indicators (RSI/EMA/entry price) were computed on a candle ~16 days old — signals on stale data
root_cause: BingX /klines returns candles descending by time (newest first). closes[-1] turned out to be the OLDEST candle, the series for indicators was reversed
why_architecture: You can't trust the element order from an external API — it's not guaranteed and can silently break all analytics
fix: Sort klines by time (old→new) before computing: data.sort(key=lambda k: int(k['time']))
prevention: Don't trust candle/series order from an API — always sort explicitly by time before indicators
cause: klines from BingX weren't sorted, the order was the reverse of what was expected

title: Env override (SCREENER_INTERVAL_H=1) lost on a serviceConnect redeploy → silent behavior change
symptom: The trader sent signals hourly, then 'went quiet' (every 4h) with no visible code changes
root_cause: Prod ran the hourly scan via env override =1 (code default =4). After a redeploy (serviceConnect) the override was lost → the default of 4h took over
why_architecture: Behavior tied to an env override ≠ code default silently changes when the variable is lost (reconnect/recreate of the service)
fix: Return the needed value as the code default (=1), don't rely on the env override alone; after serviceConnect verify the variables
prevention: Don't make critical behavior depend on an env override different from the code default. After a service reconnect — reconcile env
cause: The env var SCREENER_INTERVAL_H was lost when the service reconnected

title: Lesson dedup set empty → a normal post_lessons would flood the group with old ones
symptom: Before posting new lessons: office:lessons:posted_ids is empty, though 40 old lessons are already in the group
root_cause: post_lesson marked dedup with a title hash, while post_lessons dedups by numeric ids — the sets diverged; plus Redis could have been cleared. Running post_lessons would re-post all 40
fix: Post only truly new lessons, and after publishing set their ids in office:lessons:posted_ids; on backfill — fill the set with all existing ids
prevention: Before batch-posting lessons check the dedup set size vs the lesson count; a single dedup key (numeric ids) for all publish paths
why_architecture: Publish dedup is tied to a Redis set of ids. An empty/desynced set = a mass repeat in the group
cause: post_lesson marked dedup with a title hash, while post_lessons dedups by numeric ids — the sets diverged; plus Redis could have been cleared. Running post_lessons would re-post all 40

title: The trader was silent since Friday — market quiet was indistinguishable from a breakage
symptom: Not a single signal since Friday; unclear whether the bot works, scans, or the market is just flat
root_cause: A cascade of silent failures: (1) sending a signal depended on a Redis write — Redis down = silence, while logs/health lied 'sent'; (2) the screener took candles only from BingX and swallowed any error (except: return []), so a BingX outage/geo-ban = forever 'no signals' with no alert; (3) screener_loop started only with TRADING_CHANNEL_ID, otherwise silently didn't start; (4) /health always returned 200 even when degraded, watchdog and audit checked only the code — the scanner stop was invisible
why_architecture: The whole data stack hung on one provider (BingX) and one persistence (Redis); telemetry (last_scan_age/last_signal_age) was produced but read by nobody
fix: connectors.py: fallback chain BingX→Bybit→Binance→OKX→Crypto.com; sending decoupled from Redis (liveness advances only after a real send); 'all sources unavailable' alert; startup alert if the screener is off; heartbeat every 24h; /health returns 503 when degraded; the office audit reads the reason from the /health body. GATE=9 untouched — the threshold is optimal per backtest
prevention: In ALL bots: 'no data' and 'all ok, empty' must be distinguishable; sending an alert must not depend on persistence/logging; critical data — at least 2 providers with fallback; if a service produces health telemetry, someone must read it and react (503, not 200)
cause: single-point-of-failure + silent-failure

title: Dump of all lessons into Bug Lessons — dedup lived in Redis, lessons in git
symptom: The 'post new lessons' command dumped all 52 lessons into the group at once instead of the new ones
root_cause: post_lessons deduped by numeric ids from the Redis set office:lessons:posted_ids, but the set didn't hold them: lessons were created via git/PR and via post_lesson (which wrote a title-hash to the same set, not the id). The reader compared by id → miss → the whole file was published
why_architecture: The 'posted' state lived in Redis (ephemeral, format drifted), while the lessons themselves were in git (durable). Two sources of truth → desync → flood on any Redis reset/mismatch
fix: Single source of truth — the posted_to_group flag in lessons.json itself (git). publish_pending_lessons() posts only unflagged ones, sets the flag and commits the file; called from the audit (Cilly pulls new ones herself). Bulk dump and Redis dedup removed from post_lessons/add_lessons/post_lesson
prevention: Keep idempotency on a durable single source of truth, not an ephemeral cache; the publisher always posts 'only new by a persistent flag'; an anti-flood limit per batch; one publish path with no parallel writers using different key formats
cause: The dedup state in Redis desynced from the lesson history in git

title: Auto-cleanup wiped all lessons instead of duplicates — destructive-by-default is dangerous
symptom: An auto-migration deleted all 76 lesson messages in Bug Lessons and left 2, when it should have removed only the duplicates
root_cause: Cleanup was designed as a full wipe (all_bots mode: delete all bot messages) and ran automatically at startup. The user's intent was dedup (remove repeats, keep one of each unique lesson)
why_architecture: A destructive operation was the default and in autostart. Destructive-by-default + autonomous run = high risk of irreversible loss on any mismatch between intent and implementation
fix: The all_bots mode was removed from cleanup_group; the autostart cleanup was replaced with a publish-only startup task (deletes nothing). Bug Lessons cleanup — dedup only (dedup_lessons, on explicit command). Messages restored by re-posting from lessons.json (source of truth in git): #1-51 marked pending → the publisher re-posted them one by one, no duplicates
prevention: Destructive operations (mass deletion) — NOT default and NOT in autostart; only on explicit command and in the narrowest form (dedup, not wipe). Since data is durable in git — messages are restorable, in Telegram you may delete only duplicates. Before automating deletion — reconcile with the exact intent
cause: A full wipe was the default and automatic instead of a narrow dedup

title: Trader winrate always 'no closed' — signals and outcome tracker in different keyspaces
symptom: The digest always showed 'Winrate: no closed', the open counter piled up, /history was always ⏳
root_cause: The screener writes signals to signals:list/signal:{sid}, but outcome_tracker closed only the old keyspace tilly:signals:{asset} (BTC/ETH/SOL). The screener signals (~20 coins) were closed by nobody → status forever open
why_architecture: A historical layering of two signal systems. The screener persistence wasn't connected to the outcome tracker — structurally the winrate could never be computed
fix: New coroutine screener_outcome_tracker(app): reads signals:list, replays the outcome over OHLCV (check_outcome_in_candles), sets status win/loss/expired. Backfill at startup
prevention: One source of truth for signals; when adding a new keyspace, immediately connect it to the outcome tracker and to the stats. Test: a signal with a guaranteed-hit TP → status=win
cause: The screener writes signals to signals:list/signal:{sid}, but outcome_tracker closed only the old keyspace tilly:signals:{asset} (BTC/ETH/SOL). The screener signals (~20 coins) were closed by nobody → status forever open

title: Cilly's redis_query scans only office:* — doesn't see trader signals
symptom: Cilly couldn't fetch signals:list/signal:* when asked for the trader winrate
root_cause: In redis_query the regex is limited to the office:* prefix, output is cut at 3000 chars
why_architecture: The skill was written for office keys; when extending to other bots' data this was forgotten — Cilly has a blind spot
fix: Teach redis_query / a separate handler to read arbitrary keys (signals:list, signal:*) with pagination
prevention: Make prefix filters in diagnostic tools configurable, don't hardcode one namespace
cause: In redis_query the regex is limited to the office:* prefix, output is cut at 3000 chars

title: A stale feed gave a false live winrate of 5.4% — measure strategy quality with a backtest replay
symptom: Live winrate over accumulated signals 5.4%, win count identical in both windows (an artifact)
root_cause: Old signals were generated on stale entry prices (a stale feed) → almost all losses. Real logic quality ~47-50% (backtest replay)
why_architecture: You can't judge a strategy by a live handler on historically broken data — you need a trusted metric on a fresh feed
fix: backtest_screener.py: replay screener._analyze over fresh 4h candles, outcome forward. This is the reference; bring live measurement to its methodology
prevention: Before optimizing — a trusted baseline on clean data; don't tune on a live number with a known feed bias
cause: Old signals were generated on stale entry prices (a stale feed) → almost all losses. Real logic quality ~47-50% (backtest replay)

title: Signal bot mute to text/commands — no MessageHandler + drop_pending_updates
symptom: A command in the bot's DM (run the backtest) gave no reply at all
root_cause: main() had only CallbackQueryHandler + /stats + /signals; no MessageHandler(TEXT) and no needed command. Any text was silently dropped, and drop_pending_updates=True dropped what arrived during a restart
why_architecture: The bot was designed as a one-way signal feed; interactivity wasn't planned → silence is indistinguishable from a breakage
fix: CommandHandler('backtest') with VLAD_USER_ID authorization + a fallback MessageHandler('I'm a signal bot; commands: /stats /signals /backtest')
prevention: Every bot — at least a fallback for unknown input; don't leave the user in silence
cause: main() had only CallbackQueryHandler + /stats + /signals; no MessageHandler(TEXT) and no needed command. Any text was silently dropped, and drop_pending_updates=True dropped what arrived during a restart

title: The dev-dept chain deployed to the wrong repo with no subprocess authorization
symptom: The deploy gate put /backtest into tilly-bot, which has no backtest_screener.py/screener.py → subprocess would fail 'file not found'
root_cause: The chain didn't verify the target repository; Sekky flagged CRITICAL 'no authorization — anyone can run subprocess', but the chain committed anyway
why_architecture: An autonomous chain without a hard gate on (a) the correct repo and (b) mandatory authorization of dangerous ops (subprocess) → ships knowingly broken and insecure code
fix: Redo the command into the correct repo tilly-trader; VLAD_USER_ID authorization mandatory; a CRITICAL review flag must block the commit
prevention: A reviewer's CRITICAL = a stop factor, not a warning. Before a subprocess feature — check the target files exist and authorization
cause: The chain didn't verify the target repository; Sekky flagged CRITICAL 'no authorization — anyone can run subprocess', but the chain committed anyway

title: Railway auto-deploy was off — merge to main didn't ship, prod ran old code
symptom: After a merge to main the bot didn't update; /health returned the old body, the last deploy was a week old
root_cause: The service's repoTriggers were empty (GitHub link/auto-deploy disabled). serviceInstanceRedeploy rebuilds the OLD commit — no good for new code
why_architecture: Without a repo trigger a push to main does nothing; a silent gap between 'merged' and 'deployed'
fix: serviceConnect(repo, branch=main) restores auto-deploy and ships fresh main; a one-off rollout of a commit — serviceInstanceDeployV2 + commitSha
prevention: After setting up a service check repoTriggers; confirm a deploy by fact (new /health/restart), not by the fact of a merge
cause: The service's repoTriggers were empty (GitHub link/auto-deploy disabled). serviceInstanceRedeploy rebuilds the OLD commit — no good for new code

title: The outcome-tracker backfill flooded the group at startup
symptom: After a deploy the bot sent dozens of 'SL hit' to the group at once (closing all accumulated signals)
root_cause: screener_outcome_tracker on its first pass closed all open signals and sent a message to the group for each
why_architecture: A mass backfill operation without a separate flag for group posts = an inevitable flood on the first run
fix: Killswitch GROUP_POSTS_ENABLED (env, default off): mutes all group posts while keeping the Redis record and the DM to Vlad
prevention: Any mass operation that sends messages — behind a flag/throttle; separate backfill from notifications
cause: screener_outcome_tracker on its first pass closed all open signals and sent a message to the group for each

title: Shorts are structurally weaker than longs; GATE10 is the baseline; per-symbol pruning is overfit
symptom: Over 166d of backtest SHORT ~44% WR vs LONG ~65%; overall ~50%
root_cause: The short-mode logic yields many weak GENERAL signals; pruning the worst symbols (worst5) by WR is unstable across runs (small samples)
why_architecture: The 60% goal is reachable only long-only (dropping shorts) — that's a WR vs frequency/diversification tradeoff, a business decision
fix: GATE 9→10 (env SCREENER_GATE) as a robust upgrade (47.5%→~50-52%, +0.187→+0.26R) while keeping shorts. 60% deliberately not taken
prevention: Tune parameters only on a trusted backtest; don't hardcode a per-symbol whitelist on short history (overfit)
cause: The short-mode logic yields many weak GENERAL signals; pruning the worst symbols (worst5) by WR is unstable across runs (small samples)

title: run_daily_audit — double notify_office → spam on every failed service
symptom: The Office group was flooded with 4 separate crash notifications (logger-bot, tilly-bot, filly-bot, dilly-bot) + a summary report on top — N+1 messages instead of one
root_cause: In run_daily_audit() two paths (delegation-failure and escalate+not_code_issue) called notify_office() PER SERVICE right in the loop, BEFORE the final summary report that daily_audit_loop always sends at the end
why_architecture: Info about problem bots already accumulates in lines[] and goes into the single report — a separate notify_office() inside the loop was redundant and turned every Railway FAILED status into a separate message
fix: Removed 2 await notify_office() calls (8 lines) from run_daily_audit(). Data in lines[] preserved — goes into the final summary report. Group cleaned: 17 spam messages removed via the cleanup_group intent. Commit 2505b86, main.
prevention: notify_office() inside a per-service loop is a red flag. Group notifications should be sent only once (as a summary report), never per-service in a loop

title: Handling Telegram Bot API network instability
symptom: telegram.error.NetworkError: Bad Gateway — the bot crashes when Telegram servers are unavailable
root_cause: The Telegram Bot API is temporarily unavailable (Telegram-side issues, network issues, rate-limiting). The current retry logic fires only 5 times (max 80s), then the bot crashes.
why_architecture: A fixed retry count doesn't account for Telegram being down 5+ minutes. There's no exponential backoff with a max ceiling and jitter, leading to a thundering herd and a full bot crash.
fix: 1) Increase the retry count or make it an infinite retry with a max interval; 2) Add jitter to avoid a thundering herd; 3) Log each attempt with a timestamp; 4) Use exponential backoff with a max ceiling (e.g., 5 minutes)
prevention: Don't rely on a fixed retry count — Telegram can be down 5+ minutes. Always add a max interval between attempts and log for monitoring.
cause: Insufficient handling of temporary Telegram API unavailability

title: /health lies 503 after 120s uptime — last_poll isn't updated during run_polling()
symptom: Cilly's daily audit: 'Health failed: office-dashboard:503/degraded', while deploys are SUCCESS and there are no error logs — the service is actually alive.
root_cause: A prior fix added a health gate healthy = status=='running' AND age<120s, where age = now - last_poll. last_poll was set ONCE before app.run_polling(), which blocks the thread forever and the timestamp was never updated again. After 120s any live instance went 503.
why_architecture: The health check measured not liveness but the age of a one-time timestamp. A liveness signal must be generated periodically from the same event loop that processes updates, otherwise it measures the start moment, not the current state.
fix: A heartbeat task inside the polling event loop (post_init): every 30s updates last_poll + status='running'. Loop alive → honest 200, hung/dead → honest degradation. Commit 2917af2, branch claude/debug-buttons-503-errors-fhke7r.
prevention: A timestamp-based health check MUST have a periodic updater in the same loop as the workload. A mark set once before a blocking call is an antipattern: guaranteed to go stale.

title: Signal buttons 'spin' — the Telegram updater is dead, but /health lies 200 (a blind spot)
symptom: Inline buttons on DM signals spin forever, taps don't work. Meanwhile signals keep arriving and /health returns 200 ok.
root_cause: The screener (outbound send_message) and the Telegram updater (inbound getUpdates/callbacks) are independent. When the updater dies the bot becomes a zombie: it sends signals but doesn't answer buttons (q.answer not called). /health measured ONLY scan freshness (outbound) → lied 200 → watchdog didn't redeploy.
why_architecture: Health must cover ALL critical subsystems. Liveness of the outbound channel doesn't prove liveness of the inbound one. One metric for a multi-component process = a blind spot where a zombie lives unnoticed.
fix: 1) /health returns 503 reason=updater_down when app.updater.running==False; 2) updater_supervisor: on updater death, alert the office + os._exit(1) → Railway brings it back up. Commit 7b8460d. Separately, on request, the trade enter/skip buttons were removed (22efe43) — signals are a simulation, winrate is computed automatically from price and doesn't depend on buttons.
prevention: For a process with several subsystems, health must aggregate the liveness of each (inbound + outbound + background tasks). Checking only one branch means missing the death of the rest.

title: Cilly proposed fixes for external network faults and re-opened known lessons — audit was solo, not a team effort
symptom: In the Office group Cilly kept posting '🤔 found something suspicious' + an /approve deploy_fix for telegram.error.NetworkError / httpx.ConnectError / RemoteProtocolError (Telegram-side, not our bug), and self-contradicted: '📚 known bug, applying the known fix...' immediately followed by a fresh fix proposal for the same known issue (lessons #8/#64).
root_cause: Three holes in agents/coder.py: (1) monitor_loop filtered only a narrow IGNORE_PATTERNS substring list that caught 'NetworkError while getting Updates' (polling) but not NetworkError/ConnectError/RemoteProtocolError on send, Bad Gateway, 5xx, DNS — so external faults flowed to analyze_logs→handle_bug→stage_pending('deploy_fix'); (2) a high-confidence search_lessons match only changed the notification text but still ran analyze_logs+handle_bug, so a known lesson never blocked a repeat proposal; (3) handle_bug generated the fix solo via one Opus call and staged it with no team review.
why_architecture: Cilly is the office's autonomous auditor. Without a positive 'external vs our code' classifier she treats not-our-fault transient faults as bugs and spams approval gates; without the lesson actually gating, the same known issue re-proposes every cycle; without routing through the dev-dept review chain, unreviewed code reaches the office.
fix: 1) classify_fault(error_logs) + EXTERNAL_FAULT_PATTERNS — if the root cause is an external service (Telegram/Railway API, DNS, network) and the bot is alive, monitor_loop stays SILENT (log external_fault_ignored, long cooldown), no proposal; ANALYZER_PROMPT also gets an 'external → is_bug=false' rule. 2) A high-confidence lesson match short-circuits before analyze_logs/handle_bug, dedup via lesson_applied:{service}:{lesson_id}, and the misleading 'applying the known fix' text is removed. 3) handle_bug routes the fix through run_dev_pipeline (Devvy→[Ricky‖Testi‖Sekky]→Scribbi) and proposes only after the review gate (Ricky ≠ NEEDS_FIX + compile()); run_daily_audit section-1 uses classify_fault too.
prevention: Classify faults positively (external service vs our code) and stay silent on what isn't ours; a known lesson must BLOCK a repeat proposal, not just annotate; audit fixes go through the parallel team review gate before any office proposal; never claim 'applying a fix' unless it was actually applied.
cause: Brittle substring fault filter + cosmetic lesson match + solo un-reviewed fix generation in monitor_loop/handle_bug.
